neutron emission cross sections at En = 14.1 MeV for
Preliminary data of ADX and EDX given
Description of  22136001 the correction code MUSCC3
Pulsed beam with 2 ns pulse width and 1 MHz
.Cylindrical niobium or tantalum metal samples with 3
cm diameter by 7 cm long. The samples were positioned
at a distance of 17 cm from the tritium target and at
right angle with respect to the deuteron beam line,
with the symmetry axis parallel to the beam line. The
scattering angle could be varied by rotating the
samples around the target along the 17 cm radius arc.
In this way measurements were performed for 16 angles
The total energy resolution was +/- 0.2 MeV.
NE-213 liquid scintillator of 25.4 cm diameter
by 10 cm thick, located at an angle of 85 degrees with
respect to the deuteron beam line and at a distance of
8.3 m from the tritium target. Pulse shape discrimina-
tion was employed to separate neutrons and gamma-rays.
the TOF analysis was done for the energy range 0.5 -
Scattering from hydrogen at an angle of 25 degrees
was used for absolute determination of the cross
!  with the help of MUSCC3 code, see REL-REF,
based on JENDL-3. In case that JENDL-3 would produce
double differential neutron emission spectrum in
drastic disagreement with the measured one, the
correction can be skipped and the adoption of raw,
i.e. experimental, data is recommended by authors.
This correction amounted to about 10%, except for

Correction factor are calculated using evaluated data,
however, they are still primitive. Calculation of cor-
reaction factors should be carried out again after new
version of evaluation would be published.*By authors*
